title: clematis virginiana var. campestris
intro: Clematis virginiana var. campestris, also known as Virgin's Bower or American Clematis, is a perennial flowering plant native to eastern North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, diuretic, and sedative properties, which help to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. The plant's roots and leaves can be used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that offer health benefits such as reducing menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Clematis virginiana var. campestris can be made by drying the plant material, then using it to create teas, infusions, or tinctures under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
